briefing.headline: Crank it — winter muddy water calls for steady cranking on key structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with muddy water and stable conditions, cranking presentations shine.

KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and reel. The shimmy/quiver fall is what makes the Red Eye Shad special.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: On Tennessee River-type lakes, fish lipless-crankbait depth (10 feet or less) near grass. On clear highland reservoirs (Cherokee, Percy Priest, Lanier), fish 25-45 feet in main river channels, deepwater drains, and sharp edges near bait c...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
